pi_items.metadata_value: Pie chart represented by graph_type value 1
pi_items.verified_value: In LimeSurvey, statistics_graphtype value 1 represents pie chart (0 is bar chart by default)
pi_items.source: verifier.py (lines 75-81 comment and code assume 1=Pie), web search confirms LimeSurvey uses numeric values where 1=Pie, 0=Bar

pi_items.verified_value: Survey created programmatically via LimeSurvey JSON-RPC API
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh (lines 16-95) uses JSON-RPC calls to create survey, questions, and answers via http://localhost/index.php/admin/remotecontrol

pi_items.verified_value: Verification uses direct database queries via SQL and Python JSON-RPC API result parsing
pi_items.source: export_result.sh (lines 18-64) uses limesurvey_query function for SQL queries, verifier.py (lines 28-36) loads JSON result file

privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic task in LimeSurvey that creates a survey titled 'Participatory Budgeting 2026' with four questions: a project preference question (internal title 'PROJ'), a priority score question (internal title 'SCORE'), a zip code question (internal title 'ZIP'), and a household income question (internal title 'INC'). The task requires configuring public statistics visibility to show project preference and priority score questions while strictly hiding demographic questions (zip code and household income) to protect respondent privacy. The metadata claims about question display names ('Project Preference', 'Priority Score', etc.) are contradicted by the actual question texts created in the setup script. The core privacy constraint is verified: demographic data must be hidden from public statistics. Graph type 1 represents pie chart in LimeSurvey statistics display.
